name: Great Wall of China
summary: The Great Wall of China is a series of fortifications made of stone, brick, tamped earth, and other materials, generally built along an east-to-west line across the historical northern borders of China. Stretching over 21,000 kilometers, it is one of the most impressive architectural feats in human history and a UNESCO World Heritage Site since 1987.

timeline.title: Warring States Period Construction
timeline.description: Various Chinese states during the Warring States period (475-221 BC) began building walls to protect their borders from northern invaders and rival states. The states of Qi, Yan, and Zhao each constructed their own defensive walls.

timeline.title: Qin Dynasty Unification
timeline.description: Emperor Qin Shi Huang unified China and ordered the connection and extension of existing walls to form a continuous defensive barrier against northern nomadic groups, particularly the Xiongnu. This marked the first 'Great Wall' as a unified structure.

timeline.title: Ming Dynasty Rebuilding
timeline.description: The Ming Dynasty began a massive rebuilding campaign of the Great Wall after defeating the Mongols. The most well-preserved sections visible today, including those near Beijing at Badaling and Mutianyu, were constructed during this period using brick and stone.

timeline.title: Fall of the Ming Dynasty
timeline.description: The Manchu forces breached the Great Wall at Shanhai Pass, leading to the fall of the Ming Dynasty and the establishment of the Qing Dynasty. The wall's military significance diminished as the Qing expanded their territory beyond the wall.

timeline.title: UNESCO World Heritage Designation
timeline.description: The Great Wall was designated a UNESCO World Heritage Site, recognizing its outstanding universal value as the world's largest military structure and one of the most remarkable architectural achievements in history.

timeline.title: New Seven Wonders of the World
timeline.description: The Great Wall was voted one of the New Seven Wonders of the World in a global poll organized by the New7Wonders Foundation, cementing its status as one of humanity's greatest achievements.
